subject: fix(ux): comprehensive UX improvements for mobile and web-on-mobile
body:
Addresses 19 UX issues identified during codebase review:

Web/PWA:
- Add overscroll-behavior: contain to prevent iOS Safari rubber-band
- Add safe area inset padding for notched phones (iPhone X+)
- Add focus-visible outlines for keyboard/switch navigation accessibility
- Fix Toast safe area padding on web (use reduced padding, body handles insets)
- Slim install banner padding, auto-dismiss after 10s to reclaim screen space

Mobile touch targets:
- Increase GPS button from 32px to 40px diameter
- Add minHeight: 44px to reset button, burger menu items
- Increase burger menu icon padding to 8px

Loading & feedback:
- Add animated skeleton loading cards in StationList (4 shimmer placeholders)
- Increase Toast auto-dismiss from 2.5s to 3.5s, add close button
- Add error haptics on GPS failures and station search errors

Map improvements:
- Move NavigationControl to bottom-right (avoids burger menu overlap)
- Increase cluster count text from 13px to 15px for readability
- Disable text-allow-overlap on station price labels (enables collision detection)
- Increase station label text from 10px to 11px

Search & filters:
- Show scroll indicators on native for fuel type and radius chip selectors
- Add fadingEdgeLength and extra right padding as scroll hint
- Fix placeholder text contrast (textSecondary instead of textTertiary)
- Add maxHeight: 220px to location search suggestions for small screens

Station detail:
- Add underline + chevron icon to phone number for tap affordance

Admin:
- Add pull-to-refresh to admin station list
- Add price staleness indicator (dot + label) to PriceEditor
- Revert price field to original value on save failure

Layout:
- Fix TutorialModal cramping in landscape (reduced padding, maxHeight: 85%)
- Increase empty state message text from 14px to 15px with better line-height

Tags: ux, mobile, pwa, accessibility, performance
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-improvements
Semver: minor

title: comprehensive UX improvements for mobile and web-on-mobile
